scopeNote: Re-ran the configured milestone validators after the GST source-semantics convergence fix, then re-reviewed only that scoped fix against the prior blocked GST report-owner finding. The targeted test suite, compile/typecheck, and Spotless lint check all passed, and the GST fix review confirms non-zero seeded periods now converge across the three GST endpoints without regressing report detail payloads.

appliedUpdates.description: Archived the prior round-2 scrutiny synthesis before recording the rerun outcome.
Recorded the round-3 scrutiny synthesis for the journal-and-account-core rerun after the GST source-semantics convergence fix.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the GST convergence contract for persisted companies: `/api/v1/accounting/gst/return` now uses document aggregation semantics and ignores journal-only GST entries without source documents, so parity expectations should be expressed against the document-owned accounting/report path rather than raw journal-line totals alone.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The pass review for `gst-source-semantics-convergence-hardcut` found `TaxService.generateGstReturn()` now branches to document aggregation for persisted companies (`TaxService.java:74-90`), and updated tests in `CriticalAccountingAxesIT` / `TaxServiceTest` lock in the rule that journal-only GST entries without source documents do not drive return totals even while reconciliation/report parity is preserved.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add a reusable GST three-endpoint runtime parity command to `.factory/services.yaml` right now.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected for this round because the reviewed handoffs prove the need but still describe bespoke period-specific probes against seeded runtime data; I was not yet confident enough in one stable, non-overfit command contract to codify it automatically without risking a misleading shared command.
